initiatives.name: Own the 3-year strategic plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold and evolve the multi-year roadmap that ties brand, portfolio, channel mix, and margin profile together. Partner with the COO, Director of Ecom, and Director of Sales to translate strategy into annual operating priorities. Revisit quarterly, reset annually.
initiatives.successMetric: Strategic plan documented, dated, and reviewed with LT quarterly. Annual priorities traceable to the plan. 90% of executive hires and capital commitments tie to a named strategic pillar.

initiatives.name: Steward brand and portfolio integrity
initiatives.desiredResult: Be the final guardian of what Vosges means in the market. Validate major brand, product, and customer-experience decisions against the long-term identity. Reject work that drifts the brand.
initiatives.successMetric: Every major brand, product, and channel decision reviewed against a written brand standard. Zero launches that cannot articulate how they reinforce the Vosges identity. LT brand confidence ≥4/5 quarterly.

initiatives.name: Build and retain the executive team
initiatives.desiredResult: Hire, calibrate, and hold the standard for the top layer of the organization. Coach direct reports to run their functions without CEO intervention. Make succession plans real, not theoretical.
initiatives.successMetric: Executive seats filled with A-players within 90 days of vacancy. No direct report requires more than 2 hours/week of CEO operational input. Named successor for every L2 role by year-end.

initiatives.name: Own financial stewardship and owner alignment
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold P&L ownership at the enterprise level. Partner with the COO and Finance on forecasting discipline, capital allocation, and margin protection. Make growth-vs-cash trade-offs explicit.
initiatives.successMetric: Annual budget on time with scenario ranges. Monthly close reviewed with ownership within 15 business days. Gross margin at or above plan 2026 with no year-end surprise write-downs.

initiatives.name: Represent Vosges externally
initiatives.desiredResult: Be the face of the brand with trade partners, press, key accounts, and the luxury and food community. Open doors the rest of the org cannot open. Choose external commitments that compound brand equity.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥12 strategic relationships actively cultivated in 2026. Each external commitment tied to a written expected outcome with a post-mortem completed.

skills: Brand instinct paired with commercial discipline
Financial fluency — P&L, capital structure, scenario modeling
Restraint — governs the system rather than overriding it
ceo.purpose: set the company's strategic direction, steward the brand's identity and long-term value, and ensure Vosges Haut-Chocolat grows profitably while remaining true to its founding vision of chocolate as cultural and emotional experience.
ceo.jobDesc: Define and hold the multi-year strategic roadmap. Own financial stewardship — P&L, capital structure, and investor/owner relationships. Build and lead the executive team. Act as the primary external face of the brand. Decide when to reweight the organization's focus across growth, operations, and brand. Protect the house of Vosges: what we make, what we mean, and who we serve.
ceo.note: CEO scorecard focuses on STRATEGIC leadership, not day-to-day operations.

initiatives.name: Install and run a unified operating cadence
initiatives.desiredResult: Design and hold the full company cadence — weekly LT, monthly business review, quarterly strategic review, annual planning — with standing agendas built around scorecards, the OKR dashboard, and S&OP.
initiatives.successMetric: Full cadence documented and running on time. ≥95% LT attendance. Every meeting produces written commitments with owners and dates. Zero skipped reviews in 2026.

initiatives.name: Hold operating leaders accountable to scorecards and OKRs
initiatives.desiredResult: Run the scorecard and OKR framework as a management system, not a document exercise. Review each direct report's scorecard at a set cadence. Coach leaders to run the same system with their teams.
initiatives.successMetric: Every direct report has an active scorecard and quarterly coaching review. Off-track items get a dated recovery plan within 2 weeks. ≥70% of KRs green-to-yellow by quarter-end.

initiatives.name: Protect enterprise gross margin through operating discipline
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Finance, Procurement, and Operations to protect gross margin through costing accuracy, purchase discipline, yield control, and promo control across every channel.
initiatives.successMetric: Gross margin at or above plan by channel. Zero major margin surprises after month-close. Monthly COGS variance reviews with owners and dated corrective actions.

initiatives.name: Own the end-to-end customer fulfillment promise
initiatives.desiredResult: Be accountable for the experience the customer actually receives — lead times, ship dates, quality, service recovery. Resolve systemic gaps between Sales, Ecom, Production, and Fulfillment.
initiatives.successMetric: OTIF at or above target by channel every month. Customer-facing failures tracked and trending down QoQ. Zero unresolved structural Sales–Ops gaps older than 60 days.

initiatives.name: Sponsor the capability build-out the next chapter requires
initiatives.desiredResult: Identify the 3–5 capability gaps (B2B infrastructure, forecasting, BOMs 2.0, data integration, SQF) that will limit the next stage of growth. Sponsor, resource, and finish them.
initiatives.successMetric: Capability portfolio published and reviewed quarterly. Each project has owner, dated outcome, and stop-or-finish gate. ≥4 capability projects landed in 2026 with ≤2 stalled at any point.

skills: Operating-system thinking — building cadence and holding it
Cross-functional leadership across Ops, Tech, and Customer
Ability to hold complexity without collapsing into doing the work
Strategic patience — installing systems that outlast any single leader
coo.purpose: operationalize enterprise strategy — make sure the company delivers on what it promises customers, keeps unit economics healthy, and holds every operational leader accountable to a shared set of plans, numbers, and cadences.
coo.jobDesc: Own execution across Operations, Supply Chain, Quality, Customer Service, and Technology. Partner with the CEO (peer relationship) to translate enterprise strategy into integrated operating plans with owners, dates, and measurable outcomes. Run the weekly and monthly operating cadence. Hold leaders to their scorecards. Protect margin and reliability while the company grows.
coo.note: COO scorecard focuses on STRATEGIC operating leadership — building the operating system, not doing the work.

initiatives.name: Hit the DTC revenue plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the DTC number. Build and run the merchandising and promotional calendar. Balance acquisition investment against retention. Report weekly against plan.
initiatives.successMetric: DTC revenue at or above plan by quarter. Conversion rate trending up YoY. AOV at or above plan. Monthly variance analysis documented and reviewed.

initiatives.name: Hold the 12–18 month product roadmap
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with the Senior Product Manager on a published roadmap that reflects strategic priorities (holiday, grab-and-go, wholesale, B2B). Update quarterly; balance portfolio load against team capacity.
initiatives.successMetric: Roadmap reviewed with LT quarterly. Every active concept has a named Stage-Gate status. Portfolio load balanced against capacity with no stuck concepts.

initiatives.name: Steward brand voice end-to-end
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Art Director, Marketing Manager, and CEO on brand-voice consistency across every touchpoint — site, email, packaging, paid, PR. Catch drift early.
initiatives.successMetric: Brand voice guidelines documented and referenced in every creative brief. Quarterly brand audit across channels. Zero publicly-documented off-brand incidents in 2026.

initiatives.name: Land the 2026 launch calendar on time, on brand, on margin
initiatives.desiredResult: Drive Holiday 2026, grab-and-go, and seasonal launches through the org. Own cross-functional readiness — Marketing, Sales, Ops, Fulfillment — for every launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Holiday 2026 shipped on schedule with ≥95% of SKUs hitting planned launch date. Post-launch readiness score ≥4/5 from functional partners across every launch.

initiatives.name: Run peak-season without operational failure
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Operations, NPD, and Customer Service on peak-period readiness. Pre-plan capacity, communicate cutoffs early, hold the line when the calendar says hold.
initiatives.successMetric: Holiday and Mother's Day peaks run within plan. OTIF ≥ target through peak. Zero high-profile CX failures. Post-peak debrief documented with learnings captured.

skills: DTC e-commerce leadership on Shopify Plus
Brand + product + commercial fluency in a single seat
Cross-functional leadership across creative, commercial, and operations
Data literacy — reads Shopify + GA + retention cohorts
dir-ecom.purpose: lead Vosges' direct-to-consumer Ecom + Brand engine — owning the commercial narrative, product roadmap, and customer experience that turn the Vosges story into profitable revenue.
dir-ecom.jobDesc: Own the DTC number. Steward brand voice end-to-end. Lead the product roadmap through the Senior Product Manager. Partner with the Chef / R&D Director on creative innovation. Run the marketing calendar through Marketing, Performance Marketing, and Art Direction. Run Shopify Plus DTC through the Ecom Manager. Hit revenue while protecting the brand experience.
dir-ecom.note: Consolidated leadership of Brand, Product, and DTC Ecom under a single accountable seat.

initiatives.name: Establish and lead the B2B Sales organization structure
initiatives.desiredResult: Build the foundation — Sales Ops support, defined roles (Sales Account Manager, Junior AM, Wholesale & Private Label), team cadences, and communication protocols. Keep salespeople focused on selling.
initiatives.successMetric: B2B Sales org fully operational with clear accountability. Salespeople focused exclusively on selling and relationships, not troubleshooting fulfillment.

initiatives.name: Partner with Director of Operations on SLAs
initiatives.desiredResult: Define mutual commitments between Sales and Operations — lead times, cutoffs, escalation protocols, capacity constraints. Co-lead the weekly Sales & Ops meeting.
initiatives.successMetric: Documented SLAs in place and honored by both sides. Weekly Sales & Ops meeting running with structured agenda. Zero surprise fulfillment failures from miscommunication.

initiatives.name: Reconfigure HubSpot for B2B
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Head of Technology to rebuild HubSpot with proper pipelines, stages, required fields, and win/loss tracking that gives leadership a real-time view of revenue at risk.
initiatives.successMetric: B2B pipeline live with win/loss tracking. Weekly reporting on pipeline health, conversion, and revenue at risk. Loss reasons captured on 100% of closed-lost deals.

initiatives.name: Develop standardized B2B offering and sales materials
initiatives.desiredResult: Define approved configurations, customization rules, lead times, MOQs, and pricing. Create collateral that enables confident, accurate selling without ad-hoc fulfillment risk.
initiatives.successMetric: Published B2B catalog/menu live in 2026. Salespeople can confidently commit to what we can fulfill. Ad-hoc customization requests causing fulfillment risk trending toward zero.

initiatives.name: Protect B2B revenue through risk management
initiatives.desiredResult: Build the visibility and cadence that flags at-risk orders before the customer feels it. Real-time revenue-at-risk view for leadership.
initiatives.successMetric: Fulfillment-driven sales losses down 50% vs H2 2025. Risks flagged ≥48 hours ahead of customer impact. Zero escalations requiring CEO intervention.

skills: B2B sales leadership with cross-functional influence
Balances client advocacy with operational realities
dir-sales.purpose: lead the B2B Sales organization — ensuring salespeople have the tools, processes, and support they need to capture and protect revenue, with end-to-end accountability from sale capture through fulfillment.
dir-sales.jobDesc: Build and lead the B2B Sales organization, starting with Corporate Gifting and extending into Wholesale and Private Label. Partner with Director of Operations on SLAs and co-lead weekly Sales & Ops meetings. Reconfigure HubSpot for B2B pipeline management and win/loss tracking. Develop standardized product offerings and sales materials. Keep salespeople focused on selling while the organization delivers on every commitment.
dir-sales.note: Andy's gold-standard scorecard — the structural reference for every other role.

initiatives.name: Hit retail revenue + margin plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the multi-unit P&L. Manage labor cost. Hit contribution margin by unit. Report weekly against plan with variance commentary.
initiatives.successMetric: Retail revenue at or above plan by unit. Labor-as-percent-of-revenue within target. Contribution margin at or above plan every quarter.

initiatives.name: Standardize the theatre-of-chocolate experience
initiatives.desiredResult: Codify the Vosges boutique experience — merchandising, service, tasting, signage, climate — into a playbook. Train leads to deliver every shift.
initiatives.successMetric: Boutique audit score ≥ standard at every unit. Playbook documented and referenced in training. Every lead certified on the standard.

initiatives.name: Build the retail leadership pipeline
initiatives.desiredResult: Develop leads into managers. Name successors per unit. Make careers at Vosges real for the retail bench.
initiatives.successMetric: Every boutique has a named, ready-in-6-months successor. Internal-fill rate on boutique manager openings ≥70%.

initiatives.name: Launch airport grab-and-go at O'Hare
initiatives.desiredResult: Make O'Hare the proving ground for grab-and-go. Coordinate assortment, merchandising, and ops with NPD and Product. Feed sell-through data back to the roadmap.
initiatives.successMetric: Grab-and-go live at O'Hare by peak travel. Weekly sell-through reporting live. NPD roadmap updated with O'Hare data within 30 days of launch.

initiatives.name: Run retail food safety without compromise
initiatives.desiredResult: FIFO, climate, sanitation at every unit. Train, audit, and enforce.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero climate-related product losses. Zero customer-facing food safety incidents. Audit score ≥ standard every quarter.

skills: Hiring, training, and succession in a hospitality environment
Partnership orientation with Product and Sales
regional-gm.purpose: own the P&L and customer experience of the retail boutiques — from flagship to airport — ensuring every Vosges location is a living expression of the brand, operated profitably and safely.
regional-gm.jobDesc: Drive revenue and margin across all boutiques. Hire, train, and develop boutique leadership. Standardize the 'theatre of chocolate' customer experience. Own climate control, food safety, and stock rotation at retail. Identify the next growth unit.
regional-gm.note: Consolidates retail leadership across flagship + O'Hare.

initiatives.name: Steward the scorecard-and-OKR operating system
initiatives.desiredResult: Train managers on scorecards, OKRs, and 1:1s. Make every leader use them — not as a document, as a practice. Partner with the COO and CEO on rollout.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% of LT on active scorecards with quarterly coaching. Manager certification on the system completed by Q2. Monthly adoption report to LT.

initiatives.name: Own talent acquisition end-to-end
initiatives.desiredResult: Build a repeatable intake-to-onboarding process with hiring scorecards per role. Own sourcing, screening, candidate experience, and offer.
initiatives.successMetric: 90-day new-hire retention ≥90%. Time-to-fill within target by role. Hiring manager satisfaction ≥4/5 across offers made.

initiatives.name: Launch and run Vosges Passport
initiatives.desiredResult: Cross-training program tracked by station and function. Every critical role has a named backup.
initiatives.successMetric: Every critical station ≥2 deep on trained staff. Passport tracked live in the HR system. Zero single-point-of-failure roles by year-end.

initiatives.name: Own compliance, benefits, payroll oversight
initiatives.desiredResult: EEOC, FLSA, FMLA, benefits administration, and payroll accuracy. Partner with Finance on payroll close.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero compliance findings in 2026. Benefits enrollment completed on calendar. Payroll errors ≤0.5% of runs.

initiatives.name: Lead the culture shift Old Co → New Co
initiatives.desiredResult: Coach leaders through hard conversations. Shape the rituals, recognition, and communication that reinforce accountability culture.
initiatives.successMetric: Leadership pulse-check trending up quarter-over-quarter. Exit-interview themes visibly addressed. Cultural initiatives documented and tracked.

skills: HR leadership in a manufacturing + commercial environment
Coaching skill — can sit with leaders in hard conversations
hr-manager.purpose: build and steward Vosges as a place where people can do the best work of their careers — owning talent acquisition, onboarding, development, compliance, culture, and the operating system of people management (scorecards, 1:1s, reviews).
hr-manager.jobDesc: Own end-to-end people operations: recruiting, onboarding, benefits, payroll oversight, compliance, performance management, and culture. Act as partner for every leader on scorecards, coaching, and organizational design. Own the cross-training and capability roadmap.
hr-manager.note: Connective service — reports to CEO, serves every function.

initiatives.name: Hold the 12–18 month product roadmap
initiatives.desiredResult: Publish and update the roadmap quarterly. Balance load against team capacity. Communicate changes clearly to LT and functional partners.
initiatives.successMetric: Roadmap reviewed with LT quarterly. Every active concept has named Stage-Gate status. Roadmap-to-capacity ratio within plan.

initiatives.name: Own Stage-Gate end-to-end
initiatives.desiredResult: Every concept, every gate, right stakeholders, right data, go/no-go decision. Kill concepts that shouldn't move forward — promptly and with a reason.
initiatives.successMetric: Stage-Gate throughput improving quarterly. Go/no-go decisions made on schedule with documented rationale. Post-launch post-mortems completed on 100% of launches.

initiatives.name: Deliver Holiday 2026 and grab-and-go
initiatives.desiredResult: On-time, on-spec, on-margin delivery of both portfolios. Own cross-functional readiness for every launch in the calendar.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥95% launch-date attainment across 2026. Holiday 2026 shipped to schedule. Grab-and-go live at O'Hare by peak travel.

initiatives.name: Build the NPD three-seat unit
initiatives.desiredResult: Operate as one engine with the Chef / R&D Director and the Product Operations Specialist. Make handoffs seamless end-to-end.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero concepts stalled at handoff. Stage 3→4 cycle time down vs 2025 baseline. Three-seat team on weekly cadence.

initiatives.name: Run pre-launch cross-functional readiness
initiatives.desiredResult: Brief launches 2+ weeks ahead of ship. Hold a go/no-go gate with Marketing, Sales, Ops, and Fulfillment.
initiatives.successMetric: Launch readiness score ≥4/5 from functional partners on every launch. Zero launches missing cross-functional sign-off.

skills: NPD leadership in CPG or food
Clear written and verbal communication under deadline
sr-product-mgr.purpose: lead New Product Development for Vosges — owning the portfolio strategy, product roadmap, and Stage-Gate process, and partnering with Chef / R&D Director and Product Operations to bring new products to market on time, on brand, and on margin.
sr-product-mgr.jobDesc: Define and hold the 12–18 month product roadmap. Own Stage-Gate. Partner with the Chef / R&D Director on recipe and feasibility, with Product Operations on BOMs/MRP/data, and with Marketing and Sales on launch. Be the single accountable person for every product from concept to launch.
sr-product-mgr.note: Peer to Chef / R&D Director; both report to Director of Ecom.

initiatives.name: Hit blended ROAS target
initiatives.desiredResult: Own paid social, search, and display. Drive daily channel optimization. Report weekly against target with channel-by-channel variance.
initiatives.successMetric: Blended ROAS at or above plan quarterly. Channel-level ROAS within target. Weekly reporting delivered on time.

initiatives.name: Grow paid customer acquisition
initiatives.desiredResult: Scale new-customer acquisition within target CAC. Test creative and audiences on a weekly cadence.
initiatives.successMetric: New customers acquired at or above plan quarterly. CAC at or below target. Test velocity ≥4 experiments/month.

initiatives.name: Own attribution and measurement
initiatives.desiredResult: Build the attribution model. Enforce tagging discipline. Reconcile paid reporting with Shopify revenue monthly.
initiatives.successMetric: Monthly paid-vs-actual revenue variance ≤5%. Attribution model documented and reviewed quarterly. Zero tagging gaps on active campaigns.

initiatives.name: Optimize landing and funnel conversion
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with the Ecom Manager on CRO. Test copy, creative, and page variants continuously.
initiatives.successMetric: Conversion rate up YoY on paid traffic. Funnel-stage dropoff down QoQ. Testing backlog always populated.

initiatives.name: Protect peak-season efficiency
initiatives.desiredResult: Pre-plan peak spend and creative. Avoid last-minute panic buying. Lock peak creative 30 days ahead.
initiatives.successMetric: Peak ROAS at or above non-peak baseline. Zero rushed peak creative. Peak spend delivered within plan.

skills: Analytics stack literacy (GA4, Shopify, attribution tools)
Pragmatic reporting — tells the real story
perf-marketing.purpose: own paid acquisition, CRM performance, and attribution analytics for Vosges DTC — turning marketing spend into measurable, margin-positive revenue while protecting the brand experience.
perf-marketing.jobDesc: Own paid media (Meta, Google, programmatic), SEO, affiliate, and CRM performance. Run attribution and cohort analysis. Manage external partners and vendors. Partner with Marketing Manager on campaign activation and with Ecom Manager on site conversion. Protect blended ROAS and contribution margin.
perf-marketing.note: Performance-focused counterpart to the Marketing Manager (brand + content side).

initiatives.name: Hit the Shopify Plus DTC plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Own site merchandising, promo execution, and daily trading rhythm. Partner with Performance Marketing on traffic quality.
initiatives.successMetric: DTC revenue at or above plan monthly. Site trading reviewed daily. Variance commentary documented weekly.

initiatives.name: Fix the ShipperHQ / Quick Pay / calendar stack
initiatives.desiredResult: Close the structural bug stack causing holiday CX failures. Partner with Customer Tech Ops on root-cause and resolution.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero holiday CX incidents attributable to the shipping/calendar stack. Stack documented and monitored. Playbook for peak lock-down in place.

initiatives.name: Own the merchandising calendar
initiatives.desiredResult: Site pages, collections, hero assets, pricing updates shipped on calendar date. Partner with Marketing and Art on creative.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% merch updates shipped on date. Zero missed creative handoffs. Calendar published 90 days out.

initiatives.name: Grow conversion and AOV
initiatives.desiredResult: A/B test continuously. Partner with Performance Marketing on funnel. Own upsell, cross-sell, and cart optimization.
initiatives.successMetric: Conversion rate up YoY. AOV at or above plan. Test velocity ≥2 experiments/month on site.

initiatives.name: Support B2B Shopify launch
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the DTC-side catalog, tax, and account-data integrity for the B2B launch. Partner with Director of Sales on requirements.
initiatives.successMetric: B2B Shopify live May 1, 2026 with bare-bones feature set. Catalog and tax validated. Zero data-integrity incidents in first 30 days.

skills: Cross-functional partnership with Marketing, Tech, and Ops
Attention to detail without losing the big picture
ecom-mgr.purpose: run the day-to-day of the Vosges Shopify Plus storefront — merchandising, site operations, conversion optimization, and peak-period readiness — so every customer has a reliable, on-brand shopping experience.
ecom-mgr.jobDesc: Own Shopify Plus day-to-day: product data, collections, merchandising, site content, pricing, promo setup, and peak-period readiness. Partner with Marketing and Performance Marketing on campaign landing experience. Partner with Customer Tech Operations on the checkout/fulfillment stack. Drive conversion rate and AOV.
ecom-mgr.note: Operational counterpart to the Director of Ecom — the hands on Shopify every day.

initiatives.name: Deliver the 2026 campaign calendar
initiatives.desiredResult: Build, publish, and execute the full calendar. Brief creative, CRM, paid, and organic on every campaign with 2+ weeks of runway.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% of campaigns launch on calendar date. Every campaign has a briefed hero asset and CRM plan. Post-campaign analysis completed on major campaigns.

initiatives.name: Grow email as a margin-friendly channel
initiatives.desiredResult: Own segmentation, lifecycle flows (welcome, post-purchase, winback), and promo email. Partner with Ecom on merchandising integration.
initiatives.successMetric: Email revenue share at or above plan. Lifecycle flows live and optimized. List health improving (deliverability, unsub rate).

initiatives.name: Manage organic and SEO authority
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Performance Marketing on channel rebalance. Grow organic traffic YoY through content and technical SEO.
initiatives.successMetric: Organic sessions up YoY. Keyword rank improving on priority terms. Content calendar maintained.

initiatives.name: Support NPD launches
initiatives.desiredResult: Brief launches 2+ weeks ahead. Own hero assets, PR, influencer outreach, and launch email sequence per launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero launches shipped without hero assets, PR plan, and launch email. Launch-day engagement at or above benchmark.

initiatives.name: Steward brand voice in execution
initiatives.desiredResult: Reference brand guidelines in every brief. Run quarterly audits across channels.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero off-brand incidents in 2026. Quarterly brand audits documented. Brief template includes brand-voice checkpoint.

skills: Cross-functional collaboration with creative, commerce, and NPD
marketing-mgr.purpose: plan and execute the marketing calendar — campaigns, content, email, and brand storytelling — so the right audiences hear the right story at the right time, and commercial partners have the air cover they need to hit their numbers.
marketing-mgr.jobDesc: Own campaign strategy across seasonal launches (Valentine's, Easter, Mother's Day, Holiday) and evergreen brand storytelling. Run email content, social, PR, and influencer. Partner with Art Director on creative and with Performance Marketing Manager on paid and CRM performance. Partner with Product on launch marketing. Protect the brand voice end-to-end.
marketing-mgr.note: Brand + content counterpart to Performance Marketing Manager.

initiatives.name: Steward the Vosges visual system
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the design system, component library, and brand standards. Update quarterly as the brand evolves.
initiatives.successMetric: Design system documented and adopted across teams. Component library maintained. Quarterly updates shipped on calendar.

initiatives.name: Deliver 2026 campaign and launch creative
initiatives.desiredResult: Hero assets for every campaign and launch, briefed and delivered on calendar date. Partner with Marketing and Product on scope.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% creative delivered on calendar date. Zero launches missing hero assets. Creative quality score ≥4/5 from internal review.

initiatives.name: Own packaging design direction
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Product and NPD on packaging architecture. Protect brand expression through structure, material, and print.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero off-brand packaging shipped. Packaging library maintained. Pre-launch packaging review completed on 100% of launches.

initiatives.name: Manage designer and contract bench
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold scorecards for the Designer and Contract Senior Designer. Calibrate quality weekly.
initiatives.successMetric: Creative output up QoQ at standard quality. Designer on active scorecard. Contract bench active and productive.

initiatives.name: Protect peak-season creative capacity
initiatives.desiredResult: Pre-plan peak asset load. Commission external bench before panic sets in.
initiatives.successMetric: Peak assets delivered on schedule. Zero rushed peak creative. Contract commitments locked 60 days ahead.

skills: Instinct for when to evolve the brand vs. hold the line
art-director.purpose: own the creative voice and visual identity of the Vosges brand — directing photography, design, and packaging art across every customer touchpoint so the brand is instantly recognizable and emotionally resonant.
art-director.jobDesc: Direct the visual brand across packaging, site, email, paid, retail, and PR. Manage a small internal design team plus contracted senior creative support. Run photo art direction. Partner with Marketing Manager on campaign creative and with Product on packaging design. Evolve the brand identity without breaking it.
art-director.note: Creative authority; peer to Marketing Manager under Director of Ecom.

initiatives.name: Lead hero campaign and launch creative
initiatives.desiredResult: Own design execution for every 2026 hero launch — packaging, campaign assets, brand extensions. Deliver ready-for-production files on calendar date.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% of hero launches shipped on calendar. Zero launches missing senior creative leadership. Creative quality score ≥4/5 from internal review.

initiatives.name: Own packaging design execution
initiatives.desiredResult: Lead packaging from concept through dieline, proofing, and print release. Partner with Procurement and vendors on production.
initiatives.successMetric: All packaging released on calendar date. Zero production reprints from design error. Packaging library kept current in the design system.

initiatives.name: Elevate the Vosges design system
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Art Director to expand and maintain the brand-system library — typography, color, component patterns, templates. Make it easier for the team to ship on-brand work fast.
initiatives.successMetric: Design system usage up QoQ. Component library additions shipped each quarter. Design-system adoption across Marketing, Ecom, Retail.

initiatives.name: Mentor the Designer
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold regular craft reviews with the Designer. Pair on advanced packaging and print work. Unblock Arlen-level ramp progress documented in the Designer scorecard.
initiatives.successMetric: Designer craft progression tracked and improving each quarter. At least 2 advanced projects co-led per quarter. Designer ramp milestones met on schedule.

initiatives.name: Protect peak-season quality
initiatives.desiredResult: Plan senior creative capacity across Valentine's, Mother's Day, and Holiday peaks. Lock scope and scale up intentionally, not reactively.
initiatives.successMetric: Peak creative delivered on schedule with zero last-minute rework. 60-day commitment locked ahead of each peak. Quality maintained or improved YoY.

skills: Senior-level craft across packaging, campaign, and brand
Print and production discipline — dielines, specs, vendor fluency
Design-system thinking — component patterns, templates, scale
Ownership from concept through production release
Holds the quality bar under peak-season pressure
sr-designer.purpose: be the senior creative executor on the Vosges design team — leading hero campaign, packaging, and brand-system work under the Art Director. Raise the quality ceiling on every piece of creative that ships and mentor the Designer through higher-complexity work.
sr-designer.jobDesc: Lead execution of high-visibility creative: hero campaign assets, packaging, brand-system evolution, seasonal launches. Partner with Art Director on creative direction and with Marketing, Product, and Photography on delivery. Set the craft bar for the design team and coach the Designer on advanced packaging and print work.
sr-designer.note: Senior IC under Art Director. Currently filled by Alyssa Welch on contract. Intent: convert to FTE. Peer to Designer; partners with Marketing and Product on hero campaign and packaging work.

initiatives.name: Print + packaging ramp
initiatives.desiredResult: Develop advanced expertise in print and packaging — production processes, materials, dielines, and vendor collaboration. Graduated ramp over 6 months: Observation → Participation → Full Ownership.
initiatives.successMetric: Month 1 observation complete. Month 2 participating in vendor comms. Months 3–6 fully handling packaging projects. At least 2 packaging projects owned end-to-end by Q3.

initiatives.name: Output and turnaround
initiatives.desiredResult: Increase productivity and output speed by improving prioritization, workflow planning, and deadline management. Maintain quality standards while moving faster.
initiatives.successMetric: Reduce average turnaround time on standard tasks by 15–25% within 3 months (e.g., routine email designs from 3 days → 2–2.5 days).

initiatives.name: Communication
initiatives.desiredResult: Strengthen clarity and consistency in communication. Proactive updates, timely risk flagging, and clear summaries of decisions and next steps.
initiatives.successMetric: Weekly written status updates on all active projects. Risks flagged ≥48 hours before deadlines. Team lead looped in on external vendor comms and key in-house projects.

initiatives.name: Collaboration
initiatives.desiredResult: Strengthen cross-functional collaboration by proactively engaging internal departments and external vendors throughout the design process.
initiatives.successMetric: Initiate and lead direct communication with vendors/in-house departments on at least 3–5 projects over the next three months.

initiatives.name: Systems thinking
initiatives.desiredResult: Design beyond a single deliverable — build cohesive, scalable visual systems that work across formats, channels, and time.
initiatives.successMetric: Develop at least one scalable visual system (campaign, packaging line, or template set) adopted by the broader team. Lead internal and vendor comms and own the timeline.

skills: Brand-aligned execution across digital and print
Prioritization, deadline management, and output discipline
Proactive communication with team and vendors
Growing fluency in print and packaging production
Systems thinking — scalable templates and component sets
designer.purpose: be the day-to-day design executor across Vosges touchpoints — email, site, social, packaging support, and brand extensions. Grow toward senior-level ownership of print and packaging by the end of 2026 through a structured ramp with the Art Director.
designer.jobDesc: Execute brand-aligned design across campaign, email, social, site, and supporting packaging work. Partner with the Art Director and Senior Designer on hero projects. Develop advanced craft skills in print and packaging on a staged ramp. Communicate proactively with internal stakeholders and external vendors.
designer.note: IC designer under Art Director. Currently filled by Arlen Cornejo. Initiatives sourced from the 2026 development scorecard held with Coach Kelsey Craig.

initiatives.name: Deliver the R&D pipeline on-time, on-brand
initiatives.desiredResult: Move assigned concepts from prototype to Stage 4 on Stage-Gate timelines. Partner with the Senior Product Manager on prioritization.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% Stage-4 delivery on schedule. Zero concepts stuck in prototype >90 days. Stage-Gate rhythm held.

initiatives.name: Own ingredient evaluation and flavor voice
initiatives.desiredResult: Maintain an active ingredient shortlist. Partner with Procurement on availability, cost, and lead times.
initiatives.successMetric: Shortlist refreshed quarterly with 20+ active candidates. Zero launches delayed by ingredient sourcing surprises.

initiatives.name: Run the taste-test program
initiatives.desiredResult: Internal and customer panels with structured capture. Data, not opinion, drives go/no-go decisions.
initiatives.successMetric: Every Stage-2+ concept taste-tested. Panel data captured in the NPD system. Decisions documented with rationale.

initiatives.name: Hand off cleanly to Product Operations
initiatives.desiredResult: Full tech handoff on every concept — recipe, BOM input, spec ranges, process notes, pack-out. No tribal knowledge left behind.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero ramp issues from handoff gaps. Post-handoff Production debrief completed on every launch. Handoff template in use.

initiatives.name: Coach and cross-train the R&D team
initiatives.desiredResult: Development plans for every R&D team member. SOPs for core techniques. Build the bench.
initiatives.successMetric: Chef successor identified by year-end. Every R&D team member on a development plan. Core-technique SOPs documented.

skills: Master chocolatier or equivalent confectionery R&D expertise
Translates creative vision into manufacturable spec
Collaborative — builds with PM and Product Operations, not around them
chef-rd.purpose: be the creative and technical flavor authority for Vosges — developing recipes, prototyping new formats, and ensuring the brand's signature flavor voice carries across every new product.
chef-rd.jobDesc: Lead chocolate and confectionery R&D. Formulate new recipes. Prototype, iterate, and hand off to production. Own ingredient evaluation. Own taste tests. Partner with Senior Product Manager on the roadmap and with Product Operations on technical handoff (BOM, spec, yield).
chef-rd.note: Peer to Senior Product Manager; both report to Director of Ecom. Elevated from Chocolate R&D Chef to Director-level ownership.

initiatives.name: Restart and complete BOMs 2.0
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the migration of active BOMs into Fishbowl as source of truth. Establish the ongoing maintenance model. Close the gap opened by Kevin Brinker's departure.
initiatives.successMetric: Active SKUs migrated on schedule. Maintenance model documented and running. Zero parallel BOM spreadsheets by Q2.

initiatives.name: Own Flavor Studio and Fishbowl data integrity
initiatives.desiredResult: Monthly reconciliation on costcodes, pricing, allergens, and packaging data.
initiatives.successMetric: Drift ≤2% on costcodes monthly. Monthly reconciliation report delivered on time. Zero unresolved data discrepancies >30 days.

initiatives.name: Provide Stage-Gate costing and feasibility
initiatives.desiredResult: Costed BOMs and yield assumptions at every gate review. Partner with Procurement on sourcing assumptions.
initiatives.successMetric: Pre-launch costing within ±5% of actual. Every Stage-Gate review has a costed BOM. Zero launches surprised by COGS.

initiatives.name: Partner with Procurement on ingredient data
initiatives.desiredResult: Single source of truth in Fishbowl. Reconcile monthly with Procurement's vendor data and pricing.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero parallel spreadsheets by Q2. Monthly reconciliation clean. Ingredient data-quality score improving quarterly.

initiatives.name: Publish the NPD data scorecard
initiatives.desiredResult: Monthly readout on BOM completeness, data quality, costing accuracy, and launch readiness.
initiatives.successMetric: Scorecard metrics trending up quarterly. Monthly readout delivered to Director of Ecom and LT. Gaps closed with dated actions.

skills: ERP/MRP fluency (Fishbowl) and recipe-management systems (Flavor Studio)
Technical fluency bridging R&D and operations
Documentation-oriented — turns tribal knowledge into systems
Comfortable partnering with creative and operational stakeholders
product-ops.purpose: be the technical and data right-hand to the Senior Product Manager and Chef / R&D Director — owning BOMs, MRP, Flavor Studio, and Fishbowl data so the NPD team can move fast without breaking the data foundation the rest of the business depends on.
product-ops.jobDesc: Own BOMs, MRP logic, Flavor Studio recipe data, and Fishbowl product/costcode data for NPD. Support Stage-Gate with costing, feasibility, and data readiness. Bridge creative R&D and operational systems. Close the gap between what R&D designs and what Operations can execute.
product-ops.note: Data / systems seat in the NPD three-seat team. Closes the historical handoff gap between R&D and Operations (prior owner: Kevin Brinker, departed — BOMs 2.0 paused).

initiatives.name: Execute campaign calendar tactics
initiatives.desiredResult: Build emails, social posts, and landing updates on schedule. Partner with the Marketing Manager on briefing and QA.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% of tactics shipped on calendar date. Zero missed sends. Weekly production load delivered.

initiatives.name: Own campaign QA and trafficking
initiatives.desiredResult: Links, UTMs, render testing, and approvals before every send. Zero broken sends.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero campaign QA escapes. Every send logged and audited. QA checklist in use on 100% of sends.

initiatives.name: Support launch hero assets and briefing
initiatives.desiredResult: Assist the Marketing Manager on launch deliverables. Coordinate with Art and Product on timing and scope.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero launches missing assets. Launch-support deliverables completed on brief.

initiatives.name: Own community and influencer coordination
initiatives.desiredResult: Handle influencer shipments, tracking, and content follow-up. Maintain the influencer database.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥95% of influencer deliverables shipped on schedule. Content capture rate improving QoQ.

initiatives.name: Learn the Vosges operating system
initiatives.desiredResult: Shadow Marketing, Ecom, and Performance Marketing across the year. Build fluency across the stack.
initiatives.successMetric: Rotation completed by year-end. Development plan reviewed quarterly with Marketing Manager.

skills: Email / ESP (Klaviyo, Mailchimp, or similar) administration
jr-marketing-coord.purpose: support the Marketing Manager on campaign execution — asset trafficking, email build, social calendaring, and cross-functional coordination — creating capacity for senior marketing to focus on strategy and creative direction.
jr-marketing-coord.jobDesc: Traffic creative assets, build and schedule emails, maintain the social calendar, write first-draft copy, and coordinate with Art Direction, Performance Marketing, and Ecom on campaign execution. Own the operational details that keep every campaign on time.
jr-marketing-coord.note: Future role planned for end of 2026 as Marketing bandwidth expands.

initiatives.name: Hit B2B revenue quota
initiatives.desiredResult: Own assigned accounts. Grow existing. Prospect and close new. Maintain weekly pipeline hygiene.
initiatives.successMetric: Quota attainment ≥100% quarterly. New-logo wins at or above plan. Pipeline coverage ≥3x quarterly quota.

initiatives.name: Keep HubSpot current
initiatives.desiredResult: Every active opportunity updated weekly. Loss reasons captured on 100% of closed-lost deals. Clean stage progression.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% weekly HubSpot hygiene. Zero opportunities stuck in a stage >30 days without an update.

initiatives.name: Protect account health
initiatives.desiredResult: Proactive QBRs, retention motion, risk flagging at 90-day signal. Own the renewal conversation.
initiatives.successMetric: Account retention ≥95% YoY on assigned book. Zero retention losses without documented root cause.

initiatives.name: Sell the standardized B2B offering
initiatives.desiredResult: Use approved configurations and pricing. Escalate customization requests to the Director of Sales rather than improvising.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero fulfillment-risk customizations shipped. 100% of deals use approved configurations or documented exceptions.

initiatives.name: Partner with Ops on delivery
initiatives.desiredResult: Route orders through Sales Ops. Flag risks early. Trust the process rather than side-channel with Production.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero sales-caused fulfillment misses. Zero Production side-channels on assigned accounts.

skills: B2B sales with corporate-gifting or luxury-goods experience
Cross-functional partnership with Ops and Customer Service
Client advocate who respects operational realities
sales-account-mgr.purpose: carry a portfolio of Vosges B2B corporate-gifting accounts — capture new business, grow existing relationships, and partner with Sales Ops to deliver on every commitment.
sales-account-mgr.jobDesc: Own a named book of corporate-gifting accounts. Prospect, pitch, and close new business. Grow existing accounts. Partner with Director of Operations on fulfillment commitments and with Customer Service on recurring account needs. Maintain HubSpot pipeline discipline.
sales-account-mgr.note: Front-line seller role; scope lifted from Andy's gold-standard example.

initiatives.name: Manage assigned B2B account book
initiatives.desiredResult: Smaller account portfolio managed under Sales Account Manager coaching. Own account touches and pipeline.
initiatives.successMetric: Retention at or above target on book. Account-activity rate at or above standard.

initiatives.name: Keep HubSpot current daily
initiatives.desiredResult: Every account touch logged. Pipeline hygiene maintained weekly.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% weekly HubSpot hygiene. Daily activity logging maintained without gaps.

initiatives.name: Support proposal and order capture
initiatives.desiredResult: Build proposals using approved templates. Coordinate with Sales Ops on complex or multi-ship orders.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero proposal errors reaching customers. Proposal turnaround within target SLA.

initiatives.name: Grow selling fluency
initiatives.desiredResult: Ride along with Sales Account Manager on strategic calls. Shadow the Director of Sales on key accounts.
initiatives.successMetric: B2B catalog certification complete by Q3. Documented development plan reviewed quarterly.

initiatives.name: Own inbound lead qualification
initiatives.desiredResult: Route HubSpot inbound leads. Disqualify bad fits. Escalate qualified opportunities promptly.
initiatives.successMetric: Lead response time ≤24 hours. Qualification criteria applied consistently. Escalation SLA met.

skills: Coachable — takes feedback and applies it
jr-account-mgr.purpose: support Sales Account Managers and the Director of Sales — handling inbound leads, account research, proposal prep, and follow-up — creating capacity for senior sellers.
jr-account-mgr.jobDesc: Qualify inbound leads. Run account research. Prep proposals and quotes. Own follow-up on stalled deals. Maintain HubSpot data integrity. Shadow senior sellers to grow into a full-quota seat.
jr-account-mgr.note: Future role planned for end of 2026 as Sales scales.

initiatives.name: Build the Wholesale and Private Label pipeline
initiatives.desiredResult: Source, qualify, and close wholesale and private-label accounts. Partner with Prosper Lab on the Faire channel.
initiatives.successMetric: Pipeline value at or above plan quarterly. Closed-won revenue at or above plan. Faire account managed via Prosper Lab on plan.

initiatives.name: Own category, buyer, and broker relationships
initiatives.desiredResult: Active cadence with Faire, regional brokers, and key retail buyers. Build the relationship map.
initiatives.successMetric: Active deals with ≥10 buyers. Broker cadence maintained monthly. Relationship map current.

initiatives.name: Run the private-label program end-to-end
initiatives.desiredResult: Intake, Stage-Gate alignment, pricing, production-slot coordination, and delivery.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% of PL commitments delivered on schedule. Zero PL customer escalations for delivery or quality.

initiatives.name: Protect wholesale margin
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold pricing floors. Manage promo discipline. Monitor channel conflict with DTC and retail.
initiatives.successMetric: Wholesale gross margin at or above plan. Zero unapproved pricing exceptions. Channel-conflict incidents tracked and resolved.

initiatives.name: Partner with Director of Sales on channel strategy
initiatives.desiredResult: Weekly pipeline review. Quarterly channel plan with explicit bets and trade-offs.
initiatives.successMetric: Quarterly channel plan delivered on time. Weekly pipeline review held and documented.

skills: Wholesale sales in food or luxury CPG
Cross-functional partnership with Product and Ops
wholesale-pl.purpose: build Vosges' wholesale and private-label business — grow the Faire channel, open new retailer relationships, and develop private-label partnerships that scale revenue without diluting the brand.
wholesale-pl.jobDesc: Own wholesale account acquisition and growth. Manage the Faire relationship alongside Prosper Lab. Develop private-label opportunities with strategic partners. Partner with Product on wholesale-specific SKUs and pricing. Partner with Operations on fulfillment capacity.
wholesale-pl.note: Future role planned for end of 2026 once wholesale ops are stable and Faire channel is proven.

initiatives.name: Run the integrated Operations plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Own a rolling 13-week production, fulfillment, and capacity plan synced with Sales, Ecom, and NPD. Publish variance weekly.
initiatives.successMetric: 13-week plan variance ≤10%. Weekly readout delivered on time. Zero capacity surprises older than 30 days.

initiatives.name: Own OTIF as the Ops KPI
initiatives.desiredResult: Root-cause every miss. Eliminate repeat causes through partnership with Customer Tech Ops, Sales, and Fulfillment.
initiatives.successMetric: OTIF at or above target every month by channel. Zero repeat-cause misses beyond two occurrences.

initiatives.name: Install the weekly Sales & Ops partnership
initiatives.desiredResult: Co-lead the weekly Sales & Ops meeting with the Director of Sales. Establish and hold SLAs.
initiatives.successMetric: Weekly meeting running with structured agenda. SLAs documented and tracked. Zero CX failures from Sales–Ops gaps.

initiatives.name: Develop the floor leadership bench
initiatives.desiredResult: Coach the Ops Manager. Launch Vosges Passport on the floor. Name supervisor successors.
initiatives.successMetric: Every supervisor has a named successor by year-end. Passport live on the floor. Ops Manager on active scorecard.

initiatives.name: Partner with QA Manager on SQF readiness
initiatives.desiredResult: Share the SQF mandate. Coordinate audits, training, and incident response.
initiatives.successMetric: SQF passed with zero major NCs. Mock audits completed on schedule. Training completion at or above standard.

skills: End-to-end Operations leadership in food or CPG
People leadership, coaching, and succession planning
Quality and food-safety fluency (SQF, HACCP, GMP)
Financial literacy — cost, margin, labor, yield
dir-ops.purpose: serve as the strategic #2 to the COO for all of Operations — owning the integrated operating plan across production, fulfillment, procurement, quality, and customer service, and ensuring the factory and service stack deliver on their commitments.
dir-ops.jobDesc: Translate the COO's operating priorities into an integrated Operations plan. Run Production, Procurement, Quality, and Customer Service as one system. Be the single point of accountability for what the factory commits to and what it delivers. Build the bench beneath you.
dir-ops.note: Strategic operations leader. QA Manager and Customer Service Manager were moved under this seat from COO per the proposed structure.

initiatives.name: Hit daily and weekly production targets
initiatives.desiredResult: Run daily huddles and clean shift handoffs. Remove blockers in real time. Escalate early and cleanly.
initiatives.successMetric: Weekly plan attainment ≥90%. Daily huddles held 5x/week. Blockers resolved within shift ≥80% of the time.

initiatives.name: Lead and develop production supervisors
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold supervisor scorecards and monthly 1:1s. Cross-train to remove single-point-of-failure roles.
initiatives.successMetric: Every supervisor on active scorecard with monthly 1:1. Cross-training coverage complete by Q3.

initiatives.name: Own food safety and GMP on the floor
initiatives.desiredResult: Enforce GMP, sanitation, and allergen protocols every shift. Be the visible face of food-safety culture.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero preventable GMP findings. Daily sanitation audits held. Allergen protocol compliance at 100%.

initiatives.name: Reduce scrap and rework
initiatives.desiredResult: Root-cause the top-3 scrap drivers quarterly. Fix the source — formulation, equipment, or behavior.
initiatives.successMetric: Scrap rate down quarter-over-quarter. Top-3 driver analysis published quarterly. Fix-source actions closed.

initiatives.name: Restructure floor QA coverage with QA Manager
initiatives.desiredResult: Close the coverage gaps that let weight-variance incidents reach the customer.
initiatives.successMetric: Weight-variance incidents trending to zero. Coverage model documented and staffed. Floor QA huddle running.

skills: Supervision and coaching of line leadership
Warehouse, freezer, and preventative maintenance fluency
ops-mgr.purpose: run the day-to-day of the factory floor — production, assembly, fulfillment, warehouse, and maintenance — so that daily and weekly production targets are hit safely, consistently, and to spec.
ops-mgr.jobDesc: Be the senior presence on the floor. Own daily production huddles, shift handoffs, and execution against the production plan. Manage supervisors and their teams. Resolve operational issues in real time. Partner with Quality, Procurement, and Maintenance to keep the line running.
ops-mgr.note: Floor lead — most senior operational presence in the building day-to-day. Fulfillment, Warehouse, and Maintenance roll up here.

initiatives.name: Ship orders on time, accurately
initiatives.desiredResult: Own daily pick, pack, label, and ship throughput. Hit the calendar.
initiatives.successMetric: Ship-on-time ≥98% daily. Backlog trending to zero by end of shift. Peak throughput within plan.

initiatives.name: Own fulfillment accuracy
initiatives.desiredResult: Enforce pick and pack standards. Audit variance weekly. Close gaps with training and tooling.
initiatives.successMetric: Fulfillment accuracy ≥99%. Weekly variance audit documented. Root-cause actions closed within 14 days.

initiatives.name: Develop the pack-line bench
initiatives.desiredResult: Cross-train the team. Named backups for every station.
initiatives.successMetric: Every station ≥2 deep on trained staff. Cross-training matrix current. Zero single-point-of-failure stations.

initiatives.name: Manage peak capacity
initiatives.desiredResult: Staff peaks in advance. Coordinate with the Ops Manager on flex labor and overtime.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero peak-period backlog days. Flex labor plan in place 30 days before peak. Overtime within plan.

initiatives.name: Partner with Customer Tech Ops on ShipStation
initiatives.desiredResult: Escalate integration issues. Trend down manual touches on orders.
initiatives.successMetric: Manual ship touches down quarter-over-quarter. Zero ShipStation outages >2 hours without escalation.

fulfillment-super.purpose: lead the daily execution of Vosges' pick / pack / ship operation — hit ship-date targets, protect order accuracy, and run the team that gets every order out the door.
fulfillment-super.jobDesc: Own daily fulfillment — picking, packing, shipping — across DTC, B2B, wholesale, and retail replenishment. Manage pick/pack leads and shipping specialists. Hit ship dates. Protect order accuracy. Partner with Customer Tech Operations and Customer Service on exceptions.
fulfillment-super.note: Floor-level fulfillment leader under Ops Manager.

initiatives.name: Build vendor and sourcing strategy
initiatives.desiredResult: Segment vendors by criticality. Qualify dual sources on Class-A ingredients and packaging.
initiatives.successMetric: 2+ qualified sources for every Class-A item. Vendor segmentation documented and reviewed annually.

initiatives.name: Own inventory discipline
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold reorder points, safety stock, and turns targets tuned to the forecast.
initiatives.successMetric: Inventory turns +1 on Class-A vs 2025 baseline. Safety-stock days within target. Dead stock trending down.

initiatives.name: Run Fishbowl as the procurement source of truth
initiatives.desiredResult: 100% of POs in Fishbowl. Reconcile monthly with Flavor Studio.
initiatives.successMetric: Drift ≤2% on costcodes and pricing monthly. Zero POs outside Fishbowl. Monthly reconciliation clean.

initiatives.name: Protect holiday and launch lead times
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the procurement calendar. Run 90-day risk reviews per named launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero launch stock-outs attributable to procurement. 90-day risk review on file for every named launch.

initiatives.name: Partner with NPD on Stage-Gate sourcing
initiatives.desiredResult: Attach a sourcing feasibility assessment to every concept before Stage-Gate commit.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero launches delayed by procurement. Sourcing assessment on 100% of Stage-2+ concepts.

skills: Vendor management across food ingredients and packaging
Cross-functional partnership with NPD, Finance, Ops
Ethical sourcing and supplier audit literacy
procurement-mgr.purpose: own end-to-end sourcing, purchasing, and inventory management — making sure Vosges has the ingredients and packaging it needs at the right cost, quality, and timing, without tying up cash in excess inventory.
procurement-mgr.jobDesc: Lead sourcing and purchasing for all ingredients, packaging, and indirect materials. Manage vendor relationships. Own inventory levels, reorder points, and safety stock. Partner with NPD on sourcing for new products. Partner with Finance on cost and cash. Drive Fishbowl as the source of truth. Coach the Inventory Specialist.
procurement-mgr.note: Consolidated procurement + inventory management under a single accountable seat.

initiatives.name: Maintain Fishbowl inventory accuracy
initiatives.desiredResult: Run daily cycle counts on assigned zones. Reconcile variances within 48 hours.
initiatives.successMetric: Inventory accuracy ≥98% monthly. Cycle-count cadence held. Variances resolved within 48 hours ≥95% of the time.

initiatives.name: Own purchase-order flow
initiatives.desiredResult: PO entry, receiving reconciliation, vendor follow-up on open POs.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero orphan POs >30 days. PO-to-receipt match rate ≥98%. Vendor follow-up logged.

initiatives.name: Manage safety stock and reorder triggers
initiatives.desiredResult: Monitor reorder points daily. Flag gaps before they become stock-outs.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero Class-A stock-outs. Reorder triggers reviewed weekly. Flags escalated to Procurement Manager with 2 weeks' lead.

initiatives.name: Run monthly inventory reporting
initiatives.desiredResult: Closing inventory, aging, turns, and slow-movers. Deliver to the Procurement Manager and Finance on schedule.
initiatives.successMetric: Monthly inventory report delivered on time. Aging-stock trends documented. Slow-mover list actioned monthly.

initiatives.name: Support cycle count and physical inventory
initiatives.desiredResult: Lead the cycle-count program. Own the execution of the annual physical count.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero cycle-count schedule misses. Annual physical count completed with variance within standard.

skills: Cross-functional partnership with Product Operations and Ops
Comfortable on the floor and in the data
inventory-specialist.purpose: own the data discipline of Vosges inventory — cycle counts, receiving accuracy, Fishbowl integrity, and reorder-point maintenance — so the business runs on a number everyone can trust.
inventory-specialist.jobDesc: Execute cycle counts. Reconcile Fishbowl against physical inventory. Own receiving accuracy. Maintain reorder points and min/max levels. Flag inventory risks to the Procurement Manager. Partner with Product Operations on BOM data integrity.
inventory-specialist.note: Real role per Andy — reports to Procurement Manager.

initiatives.name: Pass SQF with zero major NCs
initiatives.desiredResult: Mock audits, gap closure, training, and audit execution. Own the full SQF readiness program.
initiatives.successMetric: SQF passed on schedule in 2026 with zero major non-conformances. Mock audits completed on plan.

initiatives.name: Build the floor QA coverage model
initiatives.desiredResult: Assess gaps with the Ops Manager. Staff and document the new coverage model.
initiatives.successMetric: Spec-drift incidents trending down monthly. Coverage model documented and staffed to plan.

initiatives.name: Own regulatory and labeling compliance
initiatives.desiredResult: FDA, Canadian, and Mexican requirements across every active SKU. Lead labeling audits before launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero regulatory findings or recalls. Labeling audit completed on 100% of launches pre-ship.

initiatives.name: Drive the Blame-the-Process-First culture
initiatives.desiredResult: Root-cause analysis on every quality incident. Publish learnings. Teach supervisors to see the process, not the person.
initiatives.successMetric: Repeat-cause incidents down quarterly. Root-cause published on 100% of incidents. Supervisor RCA certification complete by Q3.

initiatives.name: Partner with NPD on design-for-quality
initiatives.desiredResult: Embed QA gates at Stage 3 and Stage 4. Make specs manufacturable before launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero post-launch quality reformulations. QA sign-off on 100% of Stage 3 and Stage 4 gate reviews.

skills: Cross-functional influence with Ops and NPD
qa-manager.purpose: protect Vosges customers, team members, and brand by owning food safety, quality assurance, and regulatory compliance across the organization.
qa-manager.jobDesc: Own food safety (SQF, HACCP, GMP), quality assurance programs, and regulatory compliance (FDA, Canadian/Mexican export, allergen controls). Lead audits. Be the final authority on product holds, releases, and CAPAs. Partner with Ops, Procurement, and NPD so quality is built in, not inspected in.
qa-manager.note: Moved under Director of Operations (was COO). Consolidated leadership of the QA function.

initiatives.name: Hit CSAT, NPS, and response-time targets
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the top service KPIs. Build standards. Audit weekly.
initiatives.successMetric: First-response SLA met ≥95% of the time. CSAT at or above target. NPS trending up QoQ.

initiatives.name: Build the service playbook and training
initiatives.desiredResult: Document recurring scenarios. Certify agents through a standard onboarding program.
initiatives.successMetric: New-hire ramp time down 30% vs 2025. 100% of agents certified on the playbook. Playbook updated quarterly.

initiatives.name: Partner with Customer Tech Ops on Gorgias
initiatives.desiredResult: Co-own macros, rules, routing, and integrations. Drive resolution time through tooling rather than headcount.
initiatives.successMetric: Manual triage time down 25%. Resolution time improving QoQ. Tooling health reviewed quarterly.

initiatives.name: Channel customer voice into the business
initiatives.desiredResult: Monthly VOC readout to Ecom, NPD, Ops, and Retail. Turn complaints into systemic changes.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥3 systemic changes per quarter traceable to VOC. Monthly readout delivered on schedule.

initiatives.name: Handle escalations and protect the brand
initiatives.desiredResult: Own high-priority escalations personally. Make the customer whole or escalate internally with a dated plan.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% of escalations resolved within SLA. Zero public escalations requiring CEO intervention.

skills: Customer service leadership in a premium brand
Helpdesk / CX tooling fluency (Gorgias, Zendesk, or equivalent)
cust-service-mgr.purpose: lead the Chocolate Concierge Department so every Vosges customer — DTC, retail, and B2B follow-up — receives responsive, warm, and accurate service that reinforces the brand promise.
cust-service-mgr.jobDesc: Manage and mentor the service team. Own CSAT, NPS, response-time, and contact-deflection KPIs. Build workflows and playbooks. Handle escalations. Channel customer voice back into Product and Commerce so the same issues stop reaching the customer.
cust-service-mgr.note: Moved under Director of Operations per the proposed structure. Partners closely with Customer Tech Operations.

initiatives.name: Publish the tech roadmap and architecture
initiatives.desiredResult: Document the current-state architecture. Build a 12-month roadmap tied to business outcomes, not projects.
initiatives.successMetric: Architecture and roadmap published and reviewed with LT quarterly. Every roadmap item tied to a business outcome.

initiatives.name: Make Fishbowl the operational source of truth
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Procurement, Product Operations, and Finance. Close down parallel systems and spreadsheets.
initiatives.successMetric: System drift ≤2% monthly. Zero critical parallel systems by Q3. Monthly reconciliation clean.

initiatives.name: Own integrations and stack data quality
initiatives.desiredResult: Shopify, HubSpot, Gorgias, Flavor Studio, Fishbowl healthy end-to-end. Monitor and respond within SLA.
initiatives.successMetric: Integration incidents resolved within SLA ≥95% of the time. Zero data-quality incidents >24 hours.

initiatives.name: Own security, access, and IT basics
initiatives.desiredResult: MFA, access reviews, device management, backup, and incident response. No theater — real hygiene.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero unauthorized-access incidents. Quarterly access review completed. Backup restore tested quarterly.

initiatives.name: Partner on tech-enabled improvement
initiatives.desiredResult: Help Sales, NPD, Ops, and CS pick and use the right tools. Sponsor the 3–5 highest-impact improvements.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥4 major tech-enabled improvements delivered in 2026 with documented outcomes.

skills: Technology leadership in a multi-system CPG / retail environment
Business-first orientation — leads with outcome, not tool
head-tech.purpose: own the technology backbone of Vosges — Shopify, Fishbowl, Flavor Studio, HubSpot, data integrations, infrastructure, and IT — making sure the right systems exist, work together, and evolve with the business.
head-tech.jobDesc: Own technology strategy, architecture, vendor management, and data integration. Maintain and evolve the core stack (Shopify Plus, Fishbowl, Flavor Studio, HubSpot). Own security, access, and IT operations. Coach the Customer Tech Operations seat. Partner with every functional leader on tech-enabled improvement.
head-tech.note: IT box on the org chart = this seat (per Andy). Connective service under COO.

initiatives.name: Stabilize the customer-facing tech stack
initiatives.desiredResult: Audit every tool. Close gaps. Document owners and escalation paths.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero CX failures from misconfiguration by Q3. Tool inventory current. Escalation paths documented.

initiatives.name: Own Gorgias and HubSpot service configs
initiatives.desiredResult: Macros, rules, pipelines, and integrations. Quarterly health check on each tool.
initiatives.successMetric: Resolution time improving quarterly. Quarterly health check documented. Zero config-caused outages.

initiatives.name: Fix the ShipperHQ / Quick Pay / calendar chain
initiatives.desiredResult: Close the loop with the Ecom Manager — resolve or replace. Document peak-period lock-down.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero shipping-confusion tickets in Q4. Peak lock-down documented and held. Playbook for regression.

initiatives.name: Own stack data quality
initiatives.desiredResult: Accurate customer and order flow across Shopify, HubSpot, Fishbowl, and Gorgias.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero CX failures from data drift. Monthly data-quality report delivered. Drift resolved within 7 days.

initiatives.name: Support B2B Shopify and Zest multi-ship
initiatives.desiredResult: Launch configuration. Zest splitter integration across Shopify, ShipStation, and Fishbowl.
initiatives.successMetric: Zest accuracy ≥99% post-launch. B2B Shopify stable through first 30 days. Integration monitoring live.

skills: Cross-functional partnership with CS, Ecom, Sales, and IT
Pragmatic problem-solver — makes systems work, not perfect
cust-tech-ops.purpose: own the successful implementation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of all the technologies Vosges uses to interact with customers — Gorgias, HubSpot, Shopify customer-facing integrations, Zest, ShipperHQ, and the order stack — so the customer experience is reliable and the business can move fast.
cust-tech-ops.jobDesc: Own the customer-facing technology stack: Gorgias, HubSpot (service side), Shopify customer-facing integrations, Zest, ShipperHQ, and the glue between them. Configure, maintain, and improve each tool. Own integrations and data flow. Partner with Customer Service, Ecom, Sales, and Head of Technology.
cust-tech-ops.note: Reports to Head of Technology per the proposed structure.
